title: Gold Set Entry (Product-Eval)
description: One human-labeled calibration entry for the product-quality rubric (library/rubrics/product-quality-v1.md). The calibrator (src/launcher/gold-set-calibrator.js) reads these entries + model-produced labels for the same cycle and computes quadratic-weighted Cohen's Kappa per dimension. Entries are human-authored and blocklisted from self-improve (see rouge.config.json). Schema v1 targets the 6-dimension product-quality rubric; other rubrics would get their own schema.

schema_version.description: Version tag so downstream consumers can branch on entry shape.

id.description: Stable identifier — e.g. 'proj-foo-cycle-7'. Used to pair with model-produced labels.

rubric_id.description: Which rubric the labels apply to. Only product-quality in v1.

rubric_version.description: Rubric version the labeler scored against. Calibrator skips entries where rubric_version doesn't match the rubric currently in play.

source.description: Provenance of the cycle this entry was derived from.

cycle_context_excerpt.description: Optional inline snapshot of the cycle_context fields relevant to scoring (product_walk, code_review_report, active_spec, etc.). May be null if the full cycle is stored separately; the calibrator only needs the labels and ID for Kappa, the excerpt is for human reviewers.

human_labels.description: Scores for each rubric dimension. 0-3 ordinal; null = abstain (evidence insufficient — excluded from Kappa for that dimension).

human_verdict.description: Labeler's overall verdict. Aggregates with the rubric rules but may override (e.g. labeler knows a dimension=0 is truly env_limited).

labeler.description: Who labeled this entry. Single labeler in v1; multi-labeler + inter-rater reliability is v2.

labeled_at.description: When the label was assigned (ISO 8601 date-time).

notes.description: Free-text rationale — why these scores. Read by humans reviewing whether a Kappa regression reflects real drift or labeler drift.

dimensionScore.description: 0-3 ordinal score, or null to abstain. Abstain means evidence didn't resolve — dimension excluded from Kappa for this entry.
